<span id="cb18-43"><a href="#cb18-43" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">panel.grid.minor =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb18-44"><a href="#cb18-44" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  ) <span class="sc">+</span> </span>
<span id="cb18-45"><a href="#cb18-45" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">theme</span>(<span class="at">plot.title=</span><span class="fu">element_text</span>(<span class="at">family=</span><span class="st">&quot;Open Sans Condensed Bold&quot;</span>, <span class="at">margin=</span><span class="fu">margin</span>(<span class="at">b=</span><span class="dv">15</span>)))<span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb18-46"><a href="#cb18-46" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">theme</span>(<span class="at">plot.subtitle=</span><span class="fu">element_text</span>(<span class="at">family=</span><span class="st">&quot;Open Sans Condensed Light Italic&quot;</span>))<span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb18-47"><a href="#cb18-47" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">theme</span>(<span class="at">plot.margin=</span><span class="fu">unit</span>(<span class="fu">rep</span>(<span class="fl">0.5</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>), <span class="st">&quot;cm&quot;</span>))<span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb18-48"><a href="#cb18-48" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">labs</span>(<span class="at">x =</span> <span class="st">&quot;&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb18-49"><a href="#cb18-49" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">y =</span> <span class="st">&quot;&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb18-50"><a href="#cb18-50" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">title =</span> <span class="st">&quot;% of Respondents Contacted by a Political Campaign in 2020&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb18-51"><a href="#cb18-51" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">caption =</span> <span class="st">&quot;Author: Pia Deshpande, Data: 2020 Cooperative Election Study&quot;</span>)</span></code></pre></div>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64," width="672" /> There she is! The states that are missing are in stark white (notice how I did not have my scale start with white?). We see some states like Montana with a pretty high campaign contact rate, and soms states like Louisiana with a pretty low one. However, there’s a problem — we used the <code>survey</code> package to calculate these values, and they have standard errors. There’s not a good way to represent standard errors in our map as it currently exists!</p>
<p>Why are we worried about standard errors here? I was suspiciously silent about them with our first map. It’s because our first map was depicting something about CES respondents, which means our sample was <em>the same</em> as our population of interest (an incredible and rare thing). But when we analyze substantive questions, we’re usually trying to use the survey as a proxy for how a certain population behaves (in this case, the American public), which means our estimates have uncertainty to them. People who make maps and use them in their analysis are aware of this difficulty, and there are ways to overcome it that I won’t go into in this tutorial— mostly because I am not equipped to teach you something I do not know! Penn State’s Department of Geography has a good <a href="https://www.e-education.psu.edu/geog486/node/693">writeup</a> on the topic of mapping uncertainty.</p>
<p>But for our tutorial, there is one problem I might try and solve — state geography. Shy of redrawing all the state lines, I think we should make a tile map. In a tile map, all states are represented by similar sized squares, so readers will weigh them equally as they visually process them.</p>
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb19"><pre class="sourceCode r"><code class="sourceCode r"><span id="cb19-1"><a href="#cb19-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># The following code is adapted from the Urban Institute&#39;s Tutorial</span></span>
<span id="cb19-2"><a href="#cb19-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># https://urbaninstitute.github.io/r-at-urban/mapping.html#geom_tile()</span></span>
<span id="cb19-3"><a href="#cb19-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb19-4"><a href="#cb19-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># create a custom geofacet grid</span></span>
<span id="cb19-5"><a href="#cb19-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># This was constructed by the Urban Institute! I am using it here.</span></span>
<span id="cb19-6"><a href="#cb19-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># It tells R how to draw the grid, and how to name each square.</span></span>
<span id="cb19-7"><a href="#cb19-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># I am grateful someone else wrote this and not me!</span></span>
<span id="cb19-8"><a href="#cb19-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb19-9"><a href="#cb19-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>urban_grid <span class="ot">&lt;-</span> <span class="fu">tibble</span>(</span>
<span id="cb19-10"><a href="#cb19-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">row =</span> <span class="fu">c</span>(<span class="dv">1</span>, <span class="dv">1</span>, <span class="dv">2</span>, <span class="dv">2</span>, <span class="dv">2</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, </span>
<span id="cb19-11"><a href="#cb19-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">7</span>, <span class="dv">7</span>, <span class="dv">7</span>, </span>
<span id="cb19-12"><a href="#cb19-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>          <span class="dv">7</span>, <span class="dv">7</span>, <span class="dv">8</span>, <span class="dv">8</span>, <span class="dv">8</span>),</span>
<span id="cb19-13"><a href="#cb19-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">col =</span> <span class="fu">c</span>(<span class="dv">1</span>, <span class="dv">11</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">10</span>, <span class="dv">11</span>, <span class="dv">1</span>, <span class="dv">2</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">7</span>, <span class="dv">9</span>, <span class="dv">10</span>, <span class="dv">1</span>, <span class="dv">2</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">7</span>, <span class="dv">8</span>, <span class="dv">9</span>, <span class="dv">10</span>, <span class="dv">11</span>, <span class="dv">1</span>, <span class="dv">2</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">7</span>, <span class="dv">8</span>, <span class="dv">9</span>, <span class="dv">10</span>, <span class="dv">2</span>, <span class="dv">3</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">7</span>, <span class="dv">8</span>, <span class="dv">9</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">5</span>, <span class="dv">6</span>, <span class="dv">7</span>, <span class="dv">8</span>, <span class="dv">1</span>, <span class="dv">4</span>, <span class="dv">9</span>),</span>
<span id="cb19-14"><a href="#cb19-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">code =</span> <span class="fu">c</span>(<span class="st">&quot;AK&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;ME&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;WI&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;VT&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;NH&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;WA&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;ID&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;MT&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;ND&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;MN&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;IL&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;MI&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;NY&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;MA&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;OR&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;NV&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;WY&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;SD&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;IA&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;IN&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;OH&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;PA&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;NJ&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;CT&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;RI&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;CA&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;UT&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;CO&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;NE&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;MO&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;KY&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;WV&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;VA&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;MD&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;DE&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;AZ&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;NM&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;KS&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;AR&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;TN&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;NC&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;SC&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;DC&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;OK&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;LA&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;MS&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;AL&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;GA&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;HI&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;TX&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;FL&quot;</span>),</span>
<span id="cb19-15"><a href="#cb19-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">name =</span> <span class="fu">c</span>(<span class="st">&quot;Alaska&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Maine&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Wisconsin&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Vermont&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;New Hampshire&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Washington&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Idaho&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Montana&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;North Dakota&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Minnesota&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Illinois&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Michigan&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;New York&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Massachusetts&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Oregon&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Nevada&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Wyoming&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;South Dakota&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Iowa&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Indiana&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Ohio&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Pennsylvania&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;New Jersey&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Connecticut&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Rhode Island&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;California&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Utah&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Colorado&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Nebraska&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Missouri&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Kentucky&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;West Virginia&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Virginia&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Maryland&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Delaware&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Arizona&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;New Mexico&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Kansas&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Arkansas&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Tennessee&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot; North Carolina&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;South Carolina&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot; District of Columbia&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Oklahoma&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Louisiana&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Mississippi&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Alabama&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Georgia&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Hawaii&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Texas&quot;</span>, <span class="st">&quot;Florida&quot;</span>)</span>
<span id="cb19-16"><a href="#cb19-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>)</span>
<span id="cb19-17"><a href="#cb19-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb19-18"><a href="#cb19-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb19-19"><a href="#cb19-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>contact_map <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb19-20"><a href="#cb19-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb19-21"><a href="#cb19-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># remember when I defined xdimension and ydimension a while ago?</span></span>
<span id="cb19-22"><a href="#cb19-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># It&#39;s coming in handy here!</span></span>
<span id="cb19-23"><a href="#cb19-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">ggplot</span>(<span class="fu">aes</span>(<span class="at">x =</span> xdimension, <span class="at">y =</span> ydimension, <span class="at">fill =</span> contact)) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb19-24"><a href="#cb19-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb19-25"><a href="#cb19-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># We&#39;re making a tile map!</span></span>
<span id="cb19-26"><a href="#cb19-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">geom_tile</span>() <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb19-27"><a href="#cb19-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb19-28"><a href="#cb19-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># I am defining some display text here. I want</span></span>
<span id="cb19-29"><a href="#cb19-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># to print the value of &quot;contact,&quot; which is the </span></span>
<span id="cb19-30"><a href="#cb19-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># estimated percentage of Americans in a certain</span></span>
<span id="cb19-31"><a href="#cb19-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># state who were contacted by a political campaign!</span></span>
<span id="cb19-32"><a href="#cb19-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb19-33"><a href="#cb19-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># However, this number is a decimal. Another way</span></span>
<span id="cb19-34"><a href="#cb19-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># of formatting percentages is by multiplying them by 100</span></span>
<span id="cb19-35"><a href="#cb19-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># and using the round function (I wanted no decimal places).</span></span>
<span id="cb19-36"><a href="#cb19-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># I then use the paste0 function to add a pretty percentage sign!</span></span>
<span id="cb19-37"><a href="#cb19-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">geom_text</span>(<span class="fu">aes</span>(<span class="at">label =</span> <span class="fu">paste0</span>(<span class="fu">round</span>(contact<span class="sc">*</span><span class="dv">100</span>,<span class="dv">0</span>), <span class="st">&quot;%&quot;</span>)),</span>
<span id="cb19-38"><a href="#cb19-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            </span>
<span id="cb19-39"><a href="#cb19-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            <span class="co"># this makes the text white!</span></span>
<span id="cb19-40"><a href="#cb19-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>            <span class="at">color =</span> <span class="st">&quot;white&quot;</span>) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb19-41"><a href="#cb19-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb19-42"><a href="#cb19-42" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># Using our grid from before. Thanks again to the Urban Institute!</span></span>
<span id="cb19-43"><a href="#cb19-43" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># I also want to facet by state_abbv (That is, make a new tile) for</span></span>
<span id="cb19-44"><a href="#cb19-44" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># each state abbreviation.</span></span>
<span id="cb19-45"><a href="#cb19-45" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">facet_geo</span>(<span class="at">facets =</span> <span class="sc">~</span>state_abbv, <span class="at">grid =</span> urban_grid) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb19-46"><a href="#cb19-46" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">labs</span>(<span class="at">title =</span> <span class="st">&quot;Percentage of Americans Contacted by a Political Campaign in 2020&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb19-47"><a href="#cb19-47" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">subtitle =</span> <span class="st">&quot;Adapted from Code from the Urban Institute&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb19-48"><a href="#cb19-48" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">caption =</span> <span class="st">&quot;Graph by Pia Deshpande </span><span class="sc">\n</span><span class="st"> Data from the 2020 CES&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb19-49"><a href="#cb19-49" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">x =</span> <span class="cn">NULL</span>,</span>
<span id="cb19-50"><a href="#cb19-50" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">y =</span> <span class="cn">NULL</span>) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb19-51"><a href="#cb19-51" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">scale_fill_gradient</span>(</span>
<span id="cb19-52"><a href="#cb19-52" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb19-53"><a href="#cb19-53" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># Purple all the way down!</span></span>
<span id="cb19-54"><a href="#cb19-54" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">low =</span> <span class="st">&quot;plum&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb19-55"><a href="#cb19-55" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">high =</span> <span class="st">&quot;mediumpurple4&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb19-56"><a href="#cb19-56" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb19-57"><a href="#cb19-57" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># No legend title please</span></span>
<span id="cb19-58"><a href="#cb19-58" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="st">&quot;&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb19-59"><a href="#cb19-59" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>)<span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb19-60"><a href="#cb19-60" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">theme</span>(<span class="at">plot.background =</span> <span class="fu">element_rect</span>(<span class="at">colour =</span> <span class="st">&quot;white&quot;</span>),</span>
<span id="cb19-61"><a href="#cb19-61" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="at">panel.grid =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb19-62"><a href="#cb19-62" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="at">panel.grid.major =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb19-63"><a href="#cb19-63" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="at">axis.text =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb19-64"><a href="#cb19-64" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="at">axis.ticks =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb19-65"><a href="#cb19-65" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="at">axis.line =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb19-66"><a href="#cb19-66" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="at">panel.spacing =</span> <span class="fu">unit</span>(0L, <span class="st">&quot;pt&quot;</span>),</span>
<span id="cb19-67"><a href="#cb19-67" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="at">legend.position =</span> <span class="st">&quot;none&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb19-68"><a href="#cb19-68" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>        <span class="at">strip.text.x =</span> <span class="fu">element_text</span>(<span class="at">size =</span> 9L))</span></code></pre></div>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64," width="672" /> Okay! We have it! A graph-table-color thing! An abomination of nature that does fix some of our problems, but adds new ones. Readers are no longer going to see Texas as more important than Connecticut because of state size, and they can now look at the exact percentage. Data we excluded is also easy to spot! However, because this chart provides more information, it asks viewers to spend more time looking at it. This is a complicated figure—and you could argue that the color fill behind the text label doesn’t do much.</p>
<p>All maps are trade offs. In fact, even using a map is a pretty important choice! How should you decide?</p>
</div>
<div id="okay-when-should-i-use-a-map" class="section level2">
<h2>Okay, when should I use a map?</h2>
<ul>
<li>I am genuinely interested in answering a <em>geographic</em> question, and a map would help.</li>
<li>I have thought carefully about the geography I am using and whether it is appropriate. (For example, if you are interested in studying different levels of property tax, the state geography will be too broad for you. Most property taxes are decided at the local level).</li>
<li>I have thought about the trade offs of using maps and selected the best type of map. Tile maps are great when you don’t want the size of states, countries, or territories to make readers weigh larger geographic regions more importantly than small ones. On the other hand, state geography is recognizable, and can help people interpret your results. You have to make some important choices!</li>
<li>I have thought about mapping uncertainty and am either comfortable with not doing it (see our first slew of maps) or have determined how I will signal uncertainty to readers.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<div id="extra-credit-leaflet-maps" class="section level2">
<h2>Extra Credit: leaflet maps</h2>
<p>Oh, you thought we were done? Well I learned something cool recently, so I wanted to share it with y’all! What if your maps were. . . interactive?</p>
<p>Besides interactive things just being fun, they’re a great way to let your readers explore your data. In fact, they’re a great way for you to explore your data! Lucky for us the <code>leaflet</code> package has us covered. We are also going to rely on OpenStreetMap tiles (which are open source) as opposed to other—expensive—options.</p>
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb20"><pre class="sourceCode r"><code class="sourceCode r"><span id="cb20-1"><a href="#cb20-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># Code taken from: https://rstudio.github.io/leaflet/basemaps.html</span></span>
<span id="cb20-2"><a href="#cb20-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># An excellent official leaflet tutorial!</span></span>
<span id="cb20-3"><a href="#cb20-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb20-4"><a href="#cb20-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">leaflet</span>(states) <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb20-5"><a href="#cb20-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># This tells leaflet to pull from its states library</span></span>
<span id="cb20-6"><a href="#cb20-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb20-7"><a href="#cb20-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">setView</span>(<span class="at">lng =</span> <span class="sc">-</span><span class="dv">71</span>, <span class="at">lat =</span> <span class="dv">43</span>, <span class="at">zoom =</span> <span class="dv">8</span>) <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span> </span>
<span id="cb20-8"><a href="#cb20-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># This gives it a desired longitude and latitude and zoom level</span></span>
<span id="cb20-9"><a href="#cb20-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># zoom = 12 is way more zoomed in than zoom = 2 (that is,</span></span>
<span id="cb20-10"><a href="#cb20-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># larger numbers mean our map will be more zoomed in).</span></span>
<span id="cb20-11"><a href="#cb20-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb20-12"><a href="#cb20-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">addTiles</span>() <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb20-13"><a href="#cb20-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">addProviderTiles</span>(providers<span class="sc">$</span>CartoDB.Positron) <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span> <span class="co"># add muted palette basemap</span></span>
<span id="cb20-14"><a href="#cb20-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">saveWidget</span>(<span class="st">&quot;baseleaflet.html&quot;</span>) <span class="co"># save map widget</span></span>
<span id="cb20-15"><a href="#cb20-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                                 <span class="co"># this step is only important to </span></span>
<span id="cb20-16"><a href="#cb20-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                                 <span class="co"># helping us render this map on the web</span></span></code></pre></div>
<p>This is moderately exciting. You can click and drag this map! You can zoom in and out! But now what? Before we go any further, we need some data. I find <code>leaflet</code> maps work best when you have data that includes latitude and longitude. Surveys will not give you the latitude and longitude of respondents because this information may make it easy for someone to find who a particular respondent is, which is a violation of their privacy. However, there are many other contexts where interactive maps will be useful, especially when studying institutions. If you find a very interesting public opinion trend using the CES or other large survey, the next step is to try and figure out why that may be!</p>
<p>Interactive maps are also excellent at letting readers see how larger data trends may apply to their smaller communities, a particularly important job for journalists. For example, <em>The Washington Post</em> conducted a <a href="https://www.washingtonpost.com/graphics/2019/investigations/pharmacies-pain-pill-map/">detailed investigation</a> into the sales of addictive opiods in the United States. This map lets readers search their address and see how many opioids their local pharmacy sold. You can also see national trends and how your community compares to others.</p>
<p>What important question am I going to ask with my interactive map? Well, I have a friend from Seattle who has never heard of Waffle House, and I want to know why. I bet that mapping the locations of all the stores may help, so let’s load in some data! Our dataset must have latitude and longitude coordinates. Many datasets you will encounter (or create yourself) will only include addresses, which you can use to find lat and lon coordinates yourself, in a process called <em>geocoding</em>. Geocoding can be done with proprietary software like GIS, but can also be accomplished with open source tools and packages through Python or R. I won’t go into how to geocode in this tutorial, but I would encourage you to look into it if it piques your interest.</p>
<p>Back to Waffle House: this godsend of a <a href="https://github.com/minh5/wafflehouse/blob/master/webscraping/geoWaffles.csv">dataset</a> was published by Minh Mai. I will link to to Mai’s GitHub <a href="https://github.com/minh5">here</a>.</p>
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb21"><pre class="sourceCode r"><code class="sourceCode r"><span id="cb21-1"><a href="#cb21-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># Get the data here: https://github.com/minh5/wafflehouse/blob/master/webscraping/geoWaffles.csv</span></span>
<span id="cb21-2"><a href="#cb21-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># The Waffle House data has not been updated since 2015, unfortunately :(</span></span>
<span id="cb21-3"><a href="#cb21-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb21-4"><a href="#cb21-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>waffle <span class="ot">&lt;-</span> <span class="fu">read.csv</span>(<span class="fu">here</span>(<span class="st">&quot;Data/geoWaffles.csv&quot;</span>))</span>
<span id="cb21-5"><a href="#cb21-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># I am using the here() package to load this csv in with a relative file path.</span></span>
<span id="cb21-6"><a href="#cb21-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># If this is new to you, feel free to load in the data using an absolute</span></span>
<span id="cb21-7"><a href="#cb21-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># file path (however you would normally load in data), but I would</span></span>
<span id="cb21-8"><a href="#cb21-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># suggest looking into here() and using project structure in the future!</span></span></code></pre></div>
<p>I am downloading a csv here to avoid explaining a new file format, but <code>leaflet</code> maps (and other spatial mapping tools) often use geoJSONs. <a href="https://geojson.org/">Here</a> is a brief explanation of the file format. You are likely to find datasets in this format, so it is good to try and understand this particular data type! But for now, let’s stick with our waffle csv.</p>
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb22"><pre class="sourceCode r"><code class="sourceCode r"><span id="cb22-1"><a href="#cb22-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># https://learn.r-journalism.com/en/mapping/leaflet_maps/leaflet/</span></span>
<span id="cb22-2"><a href="#cb22-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb22-3"><a href="#cb22-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">leaflet</span>(waffle) <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb22-4"><a href="#cb22-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># We are telling leaflet to use the waffle dataframe</span></span>
<span id="cb22-5"><a href="#cb22-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb22-6"><a href="#cb22-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">addCircles</span>(<span class="sc">~</span>lngs,</span>
<span id="cb22-7"><a href="#cb22-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="co"># the longitude column</span></span>
<span id="cb22-8"><a href="#cb22-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             </span>
<span id="cb22-9"><a href="#cb22-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="sc">~</span>lats, </span>
<span id="cb22-10"><a href="#cb22-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="co"># the latitude column</span></span>
<span id="cb22-11"><a href="#cb22-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             </span>
<span id="cb22-12"><a href="#cb22-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="at">weight =</span> <span class="dv">3</span>, </span>
<span id="cb22-13"><a href="#cb22-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="at">radius=</span><span class="dv">40</span>,</span>
<span id="cb22-14"><a href="#cb22-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="co"># how big do we want our circles to be?</span></span>
<span id="cb22-15"><a href="#cb22-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="at">color=</span><span class="st">&quot;#ffa500&quot;</span>, </span>
<span id="cb22-16"><a href="#cb22-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="co"># what color do we want them to be?</span></span>
<span id="cb22-17"><a href="#cb22-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             </span>
<span id="cb22-18"><a href="#cb22-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="at">stroke =</span> <span class="cn">TRUE</span>,</span>
<span id="cb22-19"><a href="#cb22-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="co"># try changing this to</span></span>
<span id="cb22-20"><a href="#cb22-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="co"># stroke = FALSE and seeing</span></span>
<span id="cb22-21"><a href="#cb22-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="co"># what happens!</span></span>
<span id="cb22-22"><a href="#cb22-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             </span>
<span id="cb22-23"><a href="#cb22-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="at">fillOpacity =</span> <span class="fl">0.8</span>)  <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb22-24"><a href="#cb22-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="co"># how transparent? </span></span>
<span id="cb22-25"><a href="#cb22-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="co"># fillOpacity = 1 is opaque, </span></span>
<span id="cb22-26"><a href="#cb22-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>             <span class="co"># while fillOpacity = 0 is completely transparent</span></span>
<span id="cb22-27"><a href="#cb22-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">addTiles</span>() <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb22-28"><a href="#cb22-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">addProviderTiles</span>(providers<span class="sc">$</span>CartoDB.Positron) <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span> <span class="co"># add muted palette basemap</span></span>
<span id="cb22-29"><a href="#cb22-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">saveWidget</span>(here<span class="sc">::</span><span class="fu">here</span>(<span class="st">&quot;waffle.html&quot;</span>)) <span class="co"># save map widget</span></span></code></pre></div>
<p>Ah! Now I have a good working theory. If you’re from Seattle, you may have never seen a Waffle House before. You’ve never had that cursed experience and your life is less complete for it. (Of course, since my location dataset has not been updated since 2015, this may have changed. Perhaps I should try to collect more up to date data and run this analysis again!)</p>
<p>Plotting the locations of important buildings, businesses, and people can help us answer questions about access to resources— whether that is to polling places, primary schools, or Waffle Houses.</p>
<p>That brings us to the end of our tutorial. As always, the whole script is below (slightly rearranged so it will hopefully run smoothly on your computer). One note: you need to download the Waffle House locations onto your PC if you’d like to load that graph, without it, that section of the code will break!</p>
</div>
<div id="the-whole-script" class="section level1">
<h1>The whole script</h1>
<div class="sourceCode" id="cb23"><pre class="sourceCode r"><code class="sourceCode r"><span id="cb23-1"><a href="#cb23-1" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>knitr<span class="sc">::</span>opts_chunk<span class="sc">$</span><span class="fu">set</span>(<span class="at">echo=</span><span class="cn">TRUE</span>, <span class="at">warning=</span><span class="cn">FALSE</span>, <span class="at">message=</span><span class="cn">FALSE</span>)</span>
<span id="cb23-2"><a href="#cb23-2" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># Setting up my RmD file</span></span>
<span id="cb23-3"><a href="#cb23-3" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-4"><a href="#cb23-4" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(knitr)</span>
<span id="cb23-5"><a href="#cb23-5" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># A package for knitting things together!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-6"><a href="#cb23-6" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(htmlwidgets)</span>
<span id="cb23-7"><a href="#cb23-7" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># A package to save leaflet HTML so we can render these maps in Jekyll. If you are just looking at your maps on your local PC, you won&#39;t need to do this.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-8"><a href="#cb23-8" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># A special thanks to Rob Williams for his tutorial on how to do this: https://jayrobwilliams.com/posts/2020/09/jekyll-html</span></span>
<span id="cb23-9"><a href="#cb23-9" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-10"><a href="#cb23-10" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="do">#### LOAD PACKAGES ####</span></span>
<span id="cb23-11"><a href="#cb23-11" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(dataverse) <span class="co"># loading data</span></span>
<span id="cb23-12"><a href="#cb23-12" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(urbnmapr)  <span class="co"># geographic information for mapping!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-13"><a href="#cb23-13" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(ggplot2)   <span class="co"># pretty plots</span></span>
<span id="cb23-14"><a href="#cb23-14" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(geofacet)  <span class="co"># tile plots</span></span>
<span id="cb23-15"><a href="#cb23-15" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(scales)    <span class="co"># easy formatting</span></span>
<span id="cb23-16"><a href="#cb23-16" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(dplyr)     <span class="co"># data manipulation</span></span>
<span id="cb23-17"><a href="#cb23-17" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(survey)    <span class="co"># survey analysis</span></span>
<span id="cb23-18"><a href="#cb23-18" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(tidyverse) <span class="co"># the holy grail: more data wrangling</span></span>
<span id="cb23-19"><a href="#cb23-19" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-20"><a href="#cb23-20" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(leaflet)   <span class="co"># optional: for interactive maps!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-21"><a href="#cb23-21" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">library</span>(here)</span>
<span id="cb23-22"><a href="#cb23-22" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-23"><a href="#cb23-23" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">require</span>(<span class="st">&quot;devtools&quot;</span>)</span>
<span id="cb23-24"><a href="#cb23-24" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>devtools<span class="sc">::</span><span class="fu">install_github</span>(<span class="st">&quot;UrbanInstitute/urbnmapr&quot;</span>)</span>
<span id="cb23-25"><a href="#cb23-25" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="do">##### LOADING DATA ####</span></span>
<span id="cb23-26"><a href="#cb23-26" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>ces2020_dataverse <span class="ot">&lt;-</span> <span class="fu">get_dataframe_by_name</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-27"><a href="#cb23-27" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">filename =</span> <span class="st">&quot;CES20_Common_OUTPUT_vv.dta&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-28"><a href="#cb23-28" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">dataset =</span> <span class="st">&quot;10.7910/DVN/E9N6PH&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-29"><a href="#cb23-29" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">original =</span> <span class="cn">TRUE</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-30"><a href="#cb23-30" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">.f =</span> haven<span class="sc">::</span>read_dta,</span>
<span id="cb23-31"><a href="#cb23-31" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">server =</span> <span class="st">&quot;dataverse.harvard.edu&quot;</span></span>
<span id="cb23-32"><a href="#cb23-32" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>)</span>
<span id="cb23-33"><a href="#cb23-33" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># Let&#39;s select variables that we care about!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-34"><a href="#cb23-34" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>ces2020_selected <span class="ot">&lt;-</span> ces2020_dataverse <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb23-35"><a href="#cb23-35" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">select</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-36"><a href="#cb23-36" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb23-37"><a href="#cb23-37" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># id for each respondent</span></span>
<span id="cb23-38"><a href="#cb23-38" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    caseid, </span>
<span id="cb23-39"><a href="#cb23-39" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb23-40"><a href="#cb23-40" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># did you take the post-election survey?</span></span>
<span id="cb23-41"><a href="#cb23-41" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    tookpost, </span>
<span id="cb23-42"><a href="#cb23-42" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb23-43"><a href="#cb23-43" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># state respondent is living in.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-44"><a href="#cb23-44" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># In this code, I am both selecting</span></span>
<span id="cb23-45"><a href="#cb23-45" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># the inputstate column and renaming</span></span>
<span id="cb23-46"><a href="#cb23-46" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># it to state_fips rough, all in</span></span>
<span id="cb23-47"><a href="#cb23-47" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># one line!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-48"><a href="#cb23-48" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">state_fips_rough =</span> inputstate) <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb23-49"><a href="#cb23-49" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">mutate</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-50"><a href="#cb23-50" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb23-51"><a href="#cb23-51" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># tookpost is originally coded so 2 = Yes</span></span>
<span id="cb23-52"><a href="#cb23-52" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># and 1 = No. I am recoding it so Yes = 1</span></span>
<span id="cb23-53"><a href="#cb23-53" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># and No = 0. Why? When I do calculations, </span></span>
<span id="cb23-54"><a href="#cb23-54" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># the average of tookpost will be a proportion</span></span>
<span id="cb23-55"><a href="#cb23-55" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># between 0 and 1 that I can represent as a </span></span>
<span id="cb23-56"><a href="#cb23-56" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># percent (ex. &quot;85% of respondents took the</span></span>
<span id="cb23-57"><a href="#cb23-57" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># post-election survey&quot;).</span></span>
<span id="cb23-58"><a href="#cb23-58" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">tookpost_recoded =</span> <span class="fu">case_when</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-59"><a href="#cb23-59" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      tookpost <span class="sc">==</span> <span class="dv">1</span> <span class="sc">~</span> <span class="dv">0</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-60"><a href="#cb23-60" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      tookpost <span class="sc">==</span> <span class="dv">2</span> <span class="sc">~</span> <span class="dv">1</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-61"><a href="#cb23-61" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    ),</span>
<span id="cb23-62"><a href="#cb23-62" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb23-63"><a href="#cb23-63" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># why am I changing state_fips_rough? Like</span></span>
<span id="cb23-64"><a href="#cb23-64" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># most coding, things were breaking which inspired</span></span>
<span id="cb23-65"><a href="#cb23-65" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># me to get creative. Try running this chunk without</span></span>
<span id="cb23-66"><a href="#cb23-66" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># this line and look at ces2020_selected. See anything</span></span>
<span id="cb23-67"><a href="#cb23-67" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># weird? Check before you read on!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-68"><a href="#cb23-68" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">state_fips =</span> <span class="fu">as.character</span>(<span class="fu">sprintf</span>(<span class="st">&quot;%02d&quot;</span>, state_fips_rough))</span>
<span id="cb23-69"><a href="#cb23-69" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb23-70"><a href="#cb23-70" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># Okay, now that you&#39;ve looked, I&#39;ll spill the beans. </span></span>
<span id="cb23-71"><a href="#cb23-71" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># State FIPS codes are always two digits, like 15, 50, </span></span>
<span id="cb23-72"><a href="#cb23-72" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># and 02. R was reading in single digits like 02 as</span></span>
<span id="cb23-73"><a href="#cb23-73" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># just 2, which is a problem later on. To fix this</span></span>
<span id="cb23-74"><a href="#cb23-74" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># I used the function sprintf to tell R to add</span></span>
<span id="cb23-75"><a href="#cb23-75" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># leading zeroes to state_fips_rough until there</span></span>
<span id="cb23-76"><a href="#cb23-76" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># were two digits per entry. So a value of 4 would</span></span>
<span id="cb23-77"><a href="#cb23-77" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># turn into 04, but 36 would stay 36! I can&#39;t spend</span></span>
<span id="cb23-78"><a href="#cb23-78" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># more time on this now, but if people are interested</span></span>
<span id="cb23-79"><a href="#cb23-79" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># in this kind of data manipulation, I am happy to write</span></span>
<span id="cb23-80"><a href="#cb23-80" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># about it!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-81"><a href="#cb23-81" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  )</span>
<span id="cb23-82"><a href="#cb23-82" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-83"><a href="#cb23-83" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># hey R, for the following, use ces2020_selected please!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-84"><a href="#cb23-84" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>ces2020_selected <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb23-85"><a href="#cb23-85" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-86"><a href="#cb23-86" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># whatever calculation I tell you to do, please</span></span>
<span id="cb23-87"><a href="#cb23-87" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># do it grouped--- that is, only within entries</span></span>
<span id="cb23-88"><a href="#cb23-88" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># with the same state FIPS code.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-89"><a href="#cb23-89" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">group_by</span>(state_fips) <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb23-90"><a href="#cb23-90" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-91"><a href="#cb23-91" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># could you tell me how many entries I have per</span></span>
<span id="cb23-92"><a href="#cb23-92" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># state?</span></span>
<span id="cb23-93"><a href="#cb23-93" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">summarise</span>(<span class="at">n =</span> <span class="fu">n</span>())</span>
<span id="cb23-94"><a href="#cb23-94" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-95"><a href="#cb23-95" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-96"><a href="#cb23-96" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># you&#39;ll notice that the whole table does not print here.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-97"><a href="#cb23-97" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># To see the entire thing, print it in your R console</span></span>
<span id="cb23-98"><a href="#cb23-98" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>post_aggregate <span class="ot">&lt;-</span> ces2020_selected <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb23-99"><a href="#cb23-99" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">group_by</span>(state_fips) <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb23-100"><a href="#cb23-100" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-101"><a href="#cb23-101" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># give me the mean of tookpost_recoded </span></span>
<span id="cb23-102"><a href="#cb23-102" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># for each state, and make that a new</span></span>
<span id="cb23-103"><a href="#cb23-103" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># variable called mean_post</span></span>
<span id="cb23-104"><a href="#cb23-104" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">summarise</span>(<span class="at">mean_post =</span> <span class="fu">mean</span>(tookpost_recoded))</span>
<span id="cb23-105"><a href="#cb23-105" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-106"><a href="#cb23-106" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># let&#39;s take a look!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-107"><a href="#cb23-107" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>post_aggregate</span>
<span id="cb23-108"><a href="#cb23-108" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>post_map <span class="ot">&lt;-</span> <span class="fu">left_join</span>(post_aggregate, </span>
<span id="cb23-109"><a href="#cb23-109" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      states, <span class="at">by =</span> <span class="st">&quot;state_fips&quot;</span>)</span>
<span id="cb23-110"><a href="#cb23-110" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-111"><a href="#cb23-111" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>post_map</span>
<span id="cb23-112"><a href="#cb23-112" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># Plotting </span></span>
<span id="cb23-113"><a href="#cb23-113" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># Code adapted from: </span></span>
<span id="cb23-114"><a href="#cb23-114" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># https://www.cgoodman.com/blog/archives/2018/06/16/maps-in-r-using-urbnmapr/</span></span>
<span id="cb23-115"><a href="#cb23-115" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># I also retain some of Chris&#39; annotations here for clarity.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-116"><a href="#cb23-116" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-117"><a href="#cb23-117" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">ggplot</span>() <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-118"><a href="#cb23-118" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-119"><a href="#cb23-119" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># county map</span></span>
<span id="cb23-120"><a href="#cb23-120" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">geom_polygon</span>(<span class="at">data =</span> post_map,</span>
<span id="cb23-121"><a href="#cb23-121" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="at">mapping =</span> <span class="fu">aes</span>(<span class="at">x =</span> long, <span class="at">y =</span> lat,</span>
<span id="cb23-122"><a href="#cb23-122" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                             <span class="at">group =</span> group,</span>
<span id="cb23-123"><a href="#cb23-123" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                             </span>
<span id="cb23-124"><a href="#cb23-124" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                             <span class="co"># I want the color </span></span>
<span id="cb23-125"><a href="#cb23-125" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                             <span class="co"># that each state</span></span>
<span id="cb23-126"><a href="#cb23-126" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                             <span class="co"># has to depend</span></span>
<span id="cb23-127"><a href="#cb23-127" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                             <span class="co"># on mean_post!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-128"><a href="#cb23-128" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                             <span class="at">fill =</span> mean_post)) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-129"><a href="#cb23-129" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a> </span>
<span id="cb23-130"><a href="#cb23-130" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>   <span class="co"># add state outlines using urbnmapr</span></span>
<span id="cb23-131"><a href="#cb23-131" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">geom_polygon</span>(<span class="at">data =</span> urbnmapr<span class="sc">::</span>states,</span>
<span id="cb23-132"><a href="#cb23-132" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># when we write urbnmapr::states</span></span>
<span id="cb23-133"><a href="#cb23-133" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># we are telling R to specifically</span></span>
<span id="cb23-134"><a href="#cb23-134" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># use the urbnmapr package to pull up</span></span>
<span id="cb23-135"><a href="#cb23-135" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># states. Some coders like using this notation</span></span>
<span id="cb23-136"><a href="#cb23-136" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># consistently ---whenever they use a function</span></span>
<span id="cb23-137"><a href="#cb23-137" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># they tell R which package it comes from.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-138"><a href="#cb23-138" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># I don&#39;t do that, but if your code is</span></span>
<span id="cb23-139"><a href="#cb23-139" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># breaking and you don&#39;t know why--- try</span></span>
<span id="cb23-140"><a href="#cb23-140" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># calling on the package explicitly (sometimes)</span></span>
<span id="cb23-141"><a href="#cb23-141" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># packages have functions that are named the</span></span>
<span id="cb23-142"><a href="#cb23-142" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="co"># same thing, which confuses R!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-143"><a href="#cb23-143" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               </span>
<span id="cb23-144"><a href="#cb23-144" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="at">mapping =</span> <span class="fu">aes</span>(long, lat,<span class="at">group =</span> group),</span>
<span id="cb23-145"><a href="#cb23-145" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="at">fill =</span> <span class="cn">NA</span>, <span class="at">color =</span> <span class="st">&quot;#ffffff&quot;</span>, <span class="at">size =</span> <span class="fl">0.4</span>) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-146"><a href="#cb23-146" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-147"><a href="#cb23-147" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># projection</span></span>
<span id="cb23-148"><a href="#cb23-148" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">coord_map</span>(<span class="at">projection =</span> <span class="st">&quot;polyconic&quot;</span>)<span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-149"><a href="#cb23-149" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-150"><a href="#cb23-150" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># I want to create a scale that goes from low to</span></span>
<span id="cb23-151"><a href="#cb23-151" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># high, where the color of the high value connotes</span></span>
<span id="cb23-152"><a href="#cb23-152" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># that things are good. In the United States,</span></span>
<span id="cb23-153"><a href="#cb23-153" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># the color green is often the &quot;good&quot; or &quot;everything&#39;s</span></span>
<span id="cb23-154"><a href="#cb23-154" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># fine&quot; color, so I&#39;ll use a version of that.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-155"><a href="#cb23-155" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-156"><a href="#cb23-156" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># You can find an R Color Cheat Sheet here by Melanie Frazier: </span></span>
<span id="cb23-157"><a href="#cb23-157" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># https://www.nceas.ucsb.edu/sites/default/files/2020-04/colorPaletteCheatsheet.pdf</span></span>
<span id="cb23-158"><a href="#cb23-158" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">scale_fill_gradient</span>(<span class="at">low =</span> <span class="st">&quot;white&quot;</span>, </span>
<span id="cb23-159"><a href="#cb23-159" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="at">high =</span> <span class="st">&quot;seagreen4&quot;</span>, </span>
<span id="cb23-160"><a href="#cb23-160" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      </span>
<span id="cb23-161"><a href="#cb23-161" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="co"># I can define limits explicitly if I want.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-162"><a href="#cb23-162" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="co"># If you do this, always check that you are not</span></span>
<span id="cb23-163"><a href="#cb23-163" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="co"># accidentally excluding data! (I made sure all my)</span></span>
<span id="cb23-164"><a href="#cb23-164" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="co"># data fit in this range reasonably.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-165"><a href="#cb23-165" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="at">limits =</span> <span class="fu">c</span>(<span class="fl">0.75</span>, <span class="fl">0.95</span>), </span>
<span id="cb23-166"><a href="#cb23-166" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      </span>
<span id="cb23-167"><a href="#cb23-167" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="co"># Right now my code is in decimals.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-168"><a href="#cb23-168" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="co"># We can use a trick we learned last tutorial</span></span>
<span id="cb23-169"><a href="#cb23-169" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="co"># to make the labels into integer percentages.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-170"><a href="#cb23-170" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="co"># Try changing the accuracy = 5L argument</span></span>
<span id="cb23-171"><a href="#cb23-171" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="co"># to learn what it does!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-172"><a href="#cb23-172" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                      <span class="at">labels =</span> <span class="fu">percent_format</span>(<span class="at">accuracy =</span> 5L)) <span class="sc">+</span> </span>
<span id="cb23-173"><a href="#cb23-173" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># Theming</span></span>
<span id="cb23-174"><a href="#cb23-174" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">theme_minimal</span>(<span class="at">base_family =</span> <span class="st">&quot;Open Sans Condensed Light&quot;</span>)<span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-175"><a href="#cb23-175" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">theme</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-176"><a href="#cb23-176" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">legend.position =</span> <span class="st">&quot;right&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-177"><a href="#cb23-177" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">legend.text.align =</span> <span class="dv">0</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-178"><a href="#cb23-178" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">legend.title =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-179"><a href="#cb23-179" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">plot.margin =</span> <span class="fu">unit</span>(<span class="fu">c</span>(.<span class="dv">5</span>,.<span class="dv">5</span>,.<span class="dv">2</span>,.<span class="dv">5</span>), <span class="st">&quot;cm&quot;</span>)) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-180"><a href="#cb23-180" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="fu">theme</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-181"><a href="#cb23-181" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.line =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-182"><a href="#cb23-182" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.text.x =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-183"><a href="#cb23-183" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.text.y =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-184"><a href="#cb23-184" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.ticks =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-185"><a href="#cb23-185" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.title.x =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-186"><a href="#cb23-186" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.title.y =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-187"><a href="#cb23-187" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">panel.grid.major =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-188"><a href="#cb23-188" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">panel.grid.minor =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-189"><a href="#cb23-189" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  ) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-190"><a href="#cb23-190" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-191"><a href="#cb23-191" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># some useful titles!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-192"><a href="#cb23-192" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">labs</span>(<span class="at">x =</span> <span class="st">&quot;&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-193"><a href="#cb23-193" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">y =</span> <span class="st">&quot;&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-194"><a href="#cb23-194" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">title =</span> <span class="st">&quot;% of Respondents Who Took the Post Election Survey&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-195"><a href="#cb23-195" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">caption =</span> <span class="st">&quot;Author: Pia Deshpande, </span><span class="sc">\n</span><span class="st"> Data: 2020 Cooperative Election Study&quot;</span>)</span>
<span id="cb23-196"><a href="#cb23-196" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-197"><a href="#cb23-197" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># Plotting (Code adapted from https://www.cgoodman.com/blog/archives/2018/06/16/maps-in-r-using-urbnmapr/)</span></span>
<span id="cb23-198"><a href="#cb23-198" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-199"><a href="#cb23-199" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">ggplot</span>() <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-200"><a href="#cb23-200" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># County map</span></span>
<span id="cb23-201"><a href="#cb23-201" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">geom_polygon</span>(<span class="at">data =</span> post_map,</span>
<span id="cb23-202"><a href="#cb23-202" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="at">mapping =</span> <span class="fu">aes</span>(<span class="at">x =</span> long, <span class="at">y =</span> lat,</span>
<span id="cb23-203"><a href="#cb23-203" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                             <span class="at">group =</span> group,</span>
<span id="cb23-204"><a href="#cb23-204" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>                             <span class="at">fill =</span> mean_post)) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-205"><a href="#cb23-205" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># Add state outlines</span></span>
<span id="cb23-206"><a href="#cb23-206" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">geom_polygon</span>(<span class="at">data =</span> urbnmapr<span class="sc">::</span>states,</span>
<span id="cb23-207"><a href="#cb23-207" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="at">mapping =</span> <span class="fu">aes</span>(long, lat,<span class="at">group =</span> group),</span>
<span id="cb23-208"><a href="#cb23-208" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>               <span class="at">fill =</span> <span class="cn">NA</span>, <span class="at">color =</span> <span class="st">&quot;#ffffff&quot;</span>, <span class="at">size =</span> <span class="fl">0.4</span>) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-209"><a href="#cb23-209" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># Projection</span></span>
<span id="cb23-210"><a href="#cb23-210" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">coord_map</span>(<span class="at">projection =</span> <span class="st">&quot;polyconic&quot;</span>)<span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-211"><a href="#cb23-211" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-212"><a href="#cb23-212" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># scale_fill_gradient 2 helps us create *diverging* color scales!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-213"><a href="#cb23-213" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># I define red as a low point, white as the middle, and green</span></span>
<span id="cb23-214"><a href="#cb23-214" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># as the high point. </span></span>
<span id="cb23-215"><a href="#cb23-215" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="fu">scale_fill_gradient2</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-216"><a href="#cb23-216" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">low =</span> <span class="st">&quot;red&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-217"><a href="#cb23-217" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">mid =</span> <span class="st">&quot;white&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-218"><a href="#cb23-218" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">high =</span> <span class="st">&quot;seagreen4&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-219"><a href="#cb23-219" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-220"><a href="#cb23-220" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># I define the midpoint explicitly here! </span></span>
<span id="cb23-221"><a href="#cb23-221" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># I calculate it not as the mean of all the </span></span>
<span id="cb23-222"><a href="#cb23-222" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># state averages, but as the mean of all CES</span></span>
<span id="cb23-223"><a href="#cb23-223" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># respondents. That value will be where the</span></span>
<span id="cb23-224"><a href="#cb23-224" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># scale is a stark white.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-225"><a href="#cb23-225" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">midpoint =</span> <span class="fu">mean</span>(ces2020_selected<span class="sc">$</span>tookpost_recoded),</span>
<span id="cb23-226"><a href="#cb23-226" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">limits =</span> <span class="fu">c</span>(<span class="fl">0.75</span>, <span class="fl">0.95</span>), </span>
<span id="cb23-227"><a href="#cb23-227" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="at">labels =</span> <span class="fu">percent_format</span>(<span class="at">accuracy =</span> 5L)</span>
<span id="cb23-228"><a href="#cb23-228" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>)<span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-229"><a href="#cb23-229" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># Theming</span></span>
<span id="cb23-230"><a href="#cb23-230" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">theme_minimal</span>(<span class="at">base_family =</span> <span class="st">&quot;Open Sans Condensed Light&quot;</span>)<span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-231"><a href="#cb23-231" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">theme</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-232"><a href="#cb23-232" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">legend.position =</span> <span class="st">&quot;right&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-233"><a href="#cb23-233" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">legend.text.align =</span> <span class="dv">0</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-234"><a href="#cb23-234" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">legend.title =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-235"><a href="#cb23-235" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">plot.margin =</span> <span class="fu">unit</span>(<span class="fu">c</span>(.<span class="dv">5</span>,.<span class="dv">5</span>,.<span class="dv">2</span>,.<span class="dv">5</span>), <span class="st">&quot;cm&quot;</span>)) <span class="sc">+</span></span>
<span id="cb23-236"><a href="#cb23-236" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="fu">theme</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-237"><a href="#cb23-237" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.line =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-238"><a href="#cb23-238" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.text.x =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-239"><a href="#cb23-239" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.text.y =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-240"><a href="#cb23-240" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.ticks =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-241"><a href="#cb23-241" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.title.x =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-242"><a href="#cb23-242" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">axis.title.y =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-243"><a href="#cb23-243" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">panel.grid.major =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-244"><a href="#cb23-244" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">panel.grid.minor =</span> <span class="fu">element_blank</span>(),</span>
<span id="cb23-245"><a href="#cb23-245" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  ) <span class="sc">+</span> </span>
<span id="cb23-246"><a href="#cb23-246" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">labs</span>(<span class="at">x =</span> <span class="st">&quot;&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-247"><a href="#cb23-247" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">y =</span> <span class="st">&quot;&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-248"><a href="#cb23-248" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">title =</span> <span class="st">&quot;% of Respondents Who Took the Post Election Survey&quot;</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-249"><a href="#cb23-249" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>       <span class="at">caption =</span> <span class="st">&quot;Author: Pia Deshpande, </span><span class="sc">\n</span><span class="st"> Data: 2020 Cooperative Election Study&quot;</span>)</span>
<span id="cb23-250"><a href="#cb23-250" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-251"><a href="#cb23-251" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># Let&#39;s try making a substantive map: what percentage of respondents were contacted</span></span>
<span id="cb23-252"><a href="#cb23-252" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># by a political campaign in 2020?</span></span>
<span id="cb23-253"><a href="#cb23-253" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
<span id="cb23-254"><a href="#cb23-254" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a><span class="co"># Let&#39;s select variables that we care about!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-255"><a href="#cb23-255" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>ces2020_substantive <span class="ot">&lt;-</span> ces2020_dataverse <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb23-256"><a href="#cb23-256" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">select</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-257"><a href="#cb23-257" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb23-258"><a href="#cb23-258" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># id for each respondent</span></span>
<span id="cb23-259"><a href="#cb23-259" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    caseid, </span>
<span id="cb23-260"><a href="#cb23-260" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb23-261"><a href="#cb23-261" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># weight for survey analysis!</span></span>
<span id="cb23-262"><a href="#cb23-262" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    commonpostweight, </span>
<span id="cb23-263"><a href="#cb23-263" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb23-264"><a href="#cb23-264" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># state respondent is registered in</span></span>
<span id="cb23-265"><a href="#cb23-265" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">state_fips_rough =</span> inputstate_post, </span>
<span id="cb23-266"><a href="#cb23-266" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    </span>
<span id="cb23-267"><a href="#cb23-267" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="co"># were you contacted by a political campaign in 2020?</span></span>
<span id="cb23-268"><a href="#cb23-268" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    CC20_431a) <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb23-269"><a href="#cb23-269" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-270"><a href="#cb23-270" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">mutate</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-271"><a href="#cb23-271" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">contact =</span> <span class="fu">case_when</span>(</span>
<span id="cb23-272"><a href="#cb23-272" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      </span>
<span id="cb23-273"><a href="#cb23-273" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      <span class="co"># I am recoding this so a &quot;Yes&quot; is a 1</span></span>
<span id="cb23-274"><a href="#cb23-274" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      <span class="co"># and a &quot;No&quot; is a 0.</span></span>
<span id="cb23-275"><a href="#cb23-275" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      CC20_431a <span class="sc">==</span> <span class="dv">1</span> <span class="sc">~</span> <span class="dv">1</span>,</span>
<span id="cb23-276"><a href="#cb23-276" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>      CC20_431a <span class="sc">==</span> <span class="dv">2</span> <span class="sc">~</span> <span class="dv">0</span></span>
<span id="cb23-277"><a href="#cb23-277" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    ),</span>
<span id="cb23-278"><a href="#cb23-278" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>    <span class="at">state_fips =</span> <span class="fu">as.character</span>(<span class="fu">sprintf</span>(<span class="st">&quot;%02d&quot;</span>, state_fips_rough))</span>
<span id="cb23-279"><a href="#cb23-279" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  ) <span class="sc">%&gt;%</span></span>
<span id="cb23-280"><a href="#cb23-280" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  </span>
<span id="cb23-281"><a href="#cb23-281" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="co"># We don&#39;t want to include someone if they did not take the post-election survey</span></span>
<span id="cb23-282"><a href="#cb23-282" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a>  <span class="fu">drop_na</span>(commonpostweight)</span>
<span id="cb23-283"><a href="#cb23-283" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></a></span>
